_note: IG carousel config for chicken-bone-broth. Liquid topper recipe — imagery is more about the process and the jar-to-bowl transfer than plating.

slides.prompt: Cinematic food photography, eye-level slight-angle shot of a tall clear mason jar filled with golden-amber chicken bone broth, visibly gelatinous with small bits of collagen swirling at the surface, gently steaming. The jar sits on a weathered oak counter. Deep warm side-light from the right casting a long dramatic shadow of the jar to the left. A small rustic ladle rests beside the jar. Rustic kitchen background softly out of focus (stoneware, wooden shelves). Shot on 50mm at f/2.8, warm tungsten highlights, shallow depth of field. Home kitchen — not studio. Honest and golden, the steam is the hero. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs, no brand marks on the jar.

The bowl rests on a weathered oak floor. Long shadow stretching left. Mood: rejection, concern. Generous negative space at the top for headline overlay. No text, no labels, no logos, no captions overlaid, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic overhead flat-lay food photography with dramatic side-light from the upper right casting long warm shadows across a weathered oak surface: a pile of raw chicken bones (backs and necks) on parchment paper, a glass jar of clear cool water, a small unmarked glass bottle of apple cider vinegar, a single whole orange carrot (unpeeled) and two celery stalks lying side by side. Generous negative space between items. Warm tungsten-toned light, deep shadows. Absolutely no onion, no garlic, no salt, no seasoning. No text on anything, no writing, no watermarks, no logos, no jars with labels, no utensils, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic close-up food photography at an eye-level 25-degree angle: a large cast-iron stockpot on a stovetop, lid partially ajar, golden-amber broth visibly simmering inside with chicken bones and chopped vegetables (carrot, celery) breaking the surface. Cinematic close-up photography, 9:16 portrait, of a stream of golden-amber bone broth being poured from a small clear glass mason jar onto a cream ceramic dog bowl filled with dry kibble. The bowl rests on a weathered oak counter, long dramatic shadow stretching left. Tight crop — bowl and pour stream fill about 70% of frame. Honest, comforting — this is a real meal-topper moment. title: When Nalla Stopped Eating Her Dinner
ig_caption:
Nalla turned her nose up at her dinner. Same food she'd eaten for 4 years.

I tried mixing in store-bought toppers ($8 a week, half preservatives). Switched brands. Nothing worked.

Then I made bone broth from scratch:

🍗 4 chicken backs (or 1 carcass)
🥕 2 carrots — no onion, no garlic
🌿 1 sprig parsley
🍶 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar (pulls minerals from the bones)
💧 Water to cover

12-hour low simmer → strain → cool → fridge. She inhaled the bowl. Freezes 90 days.

I keep one in the freezer at all times now.

Save this 🔖 — picky days come with no warning.

Full recipe + amounts: link in bio

👉 What's your go-to when your dog stops eating?

#pickyeater #bonebroth #dognutrition #petparent #nallasdad #dogfoodandfun
